summary: Matched the remaining sysdolphin baselib HSD class implementation by deleting asm/sysdolphin/baselib/class.s, removing its object from obj_files.mk, and adding the missing C implementations to src/sysdolphin/baselib/class.c. The PR also moved class APIs out of the generic include/functions.h bucket into include/sysdolphin/baselib/class.h, tightened HSD_ClassInfo init callback typing, and updated callers in dobj, robj asm, and initialize to use the newly named/typed class functions.
what_changed: Deleted the 850-line assembly file asm/sysdolphin/baselib/class.s and removed asm/sysdolphin/baselib/class.s.o from obj_files.mk, leaving class.c.o as the compiled implementation.

Added C implementations for HSD class allocation/lifecycle functionality including hsdAllocMemPiece, hsdFreeMemPiece, _hsdClassAlloc, _hsdClassInit, _hsdClassRelease, _hsdClassDestroy, _hsdClassAmnesia, _hsdClassInfoInit, hsdNew, hsdIsDescendantOf, hsdObjIsDescendantOf, hsdForgetClassLibrary, hsdSearchClassInfo, and class reporting helpers visible in the diff.
Changed hsdClass initialization from an extern func_803822C0 initializer to the named _hsdClassInfoInit function and initialized HSD_ClassInfo hsdClass with that callback.
Made memory_list, nb_memory_list, and lbl_804D7708 static globals in class.c rather than extern declarations.
Replaced raw __assert calls in class.c with assert_line macros after adding sysdolphin/baselib/debug.h to class.h.
Added force_active unused strings and a dont_inline pragma around ClassInfoInit, indicating matching-sensitive handling of strings and inlining.

Moved class function declarations from include/functions.h into include/sysdolphin/baselib/class.h and made prototypes more specific, including hsdAllocMemPiece(s32), hsdFreeMemPiece(void*, s32), hsdNew(HSD_ClassInfo*), and hsdSearchClassInfo(const char*).
Changed HSD_ClassInfo.init from void-returning to int-returning so hsdNew can test init failure with if (info->init(cls) < 0).
Updated HSD_DObjLoadDesc to store hsdSearchClassInfo(desc->class_name) in a local HSD_ClassInfo* and pass it to hsdNew(info) instead of calling hsdNew with no arguments.
Updated initialize.c to call hsdForgetClassLibrary("sysdolphin_base_library") instead of func_80382718.
Updated robj.s branch label/comment target from func_803825D8 to hsdObjIsDescendantOf.
smart_moves: Removed the asm object from the build once the C matched, which prevents duplicate text and records the decompilation boundary clearly in obj_files.mk.
Localized class-system prototypes in class.h instead of leaving them as untyped/loosely typed declarations in include/functions.h.
Used assert_line and included debug.h to preserve source-line assertion behavior while reducing raw label/string plumbing.
Kept matching-sensitive pragmas explicit: dont_inline around ClassInfoInit and force_active around unused diagnostic strings.
Threaded HSD_ClassInfo* through dobj allocation after hsdSearchClassInfo, making hsdNew's typed interface usable by callers rather than relying on a no-argument placeholder.
Preserved unknown KAR-derived names with comments rather than inventing Melee symbol names where evidence was weak.
decomp_lessons: When replacing a large asm file, also audit obj_files.mk and nearby asm references so the new C object is the single implementation and existing assembly callsites point at the new symbol names.

Header cleanup is part of matching: move recovered functions from generic functions.h into the subsystem header with concrete parameter and return types as soon as the C implementation establishes them.
Callback return types matter for both correctness and matching; HSD_ClassInfo.init needed to return int because hsdNew checks for negative init failure.
For SysDolphin class code, class initialization is guarded by head.flags & 1 and often invokes head.info_init lazily before walking parents or allocating objects.
Free-list class allocation rounds object sizes to 32-byte units using expressions like (size + 0x1F) / 32 - 1; preserving this arithmetic shape can be important for matching.
Decompilers should not over-name stripped, auto-inlined Melee functions just because equivalent code exists in KAR; leave uncertainty visible or use a prefix that disambiguates source provenance.
Static globals can replace extern labels once the owning C translation unit is recovered, but keep forced-active strings/globals that affect binary layout.
naming_conventions: Recovered core class API names use hsd* camel-style names: hsdAllocMemPiece, hsdFreeMemPiece, hsdNew, hsdIsDescendantOf, hsdObjIsDescendantOf, hsdForgetClassLibrary, hsdSearchClassInfo.
Internal HSD class virtual-method implementations use leading underscore names: _hsdClassAlloc, _hsdClassInit, _hsdClassRelease, _hsdClassDestroy, _hsdClassAmnesia, _hsdClassInfoInit.
HSD struct types retain HSD_* names such as HSD_Class, HSD_ClassInfo, HSD_MemoryEntry, HSD_FreeList, and HSD_Obj.
Review feedback suggested that functions named only from Kirby Air Ride addresses should not look like Melee addresses; a suggested temporary convention was kar_804211B4.
Final diff evidence still shows KAR-address comments and names like func_80420FBC, func_804211B4, and func_804212F4, so the KAR-prefix rename was apparently deferred or not applied in this slice.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Full asm deletion: asm/sysdolphin/baselib/class.s was removed after matching its functions in class.c.
Build-list tactic: remove $(BUILD_DIR)/asm/sysdolphin/baselib/class.s.o from TEXT_O_FILES when src/sysdolphin/baselib/class.c.o covers the text.
Used #pragma dont_inline on ClassInfoInit to preserve call structure around lazy class initialization.

Used #pragma force_active for unused diagnostic strings such as "entry %d <null>
", "entry %d - %d <null>
", "entry %d(%d)", "  nb_alloc %d nb_free %d
", and "info_hash".

Kept unusual C shapes likely needed for matching, including duplicated assignment var_r31 = var_r31 = info, inline helpers HSD_GetClassInfo/HSD_PushClassInfo, and a seemingly no-op !var_r29 statement.
Updated an existing assembly callsite in robj.s to reference hsdObjIsDescendantOf without otherwise decompiling robj.s in this PR.

review_feedback.author: r-burns
review_feedback.feedback: The author noted comments were left on functions with unknown names that exist in KAR but are stripped in Melee because their only uses are auto-inlined, and suggested Psi might know what to call them.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.feedback: Suggested renaming KAR-address-derived functions to a form like kar_804211B4 so readers would not think the address was a Melee address.

review_feedback.author: r-burns
review_feedback.feedback: Said they would make a new issue so the PR could be merged, implying the uncertain KAR-derived naming was accepted as follow-up rather than blocking the match.

follow_up_queries: Was a later issue or PR created to rename KAR-address-derived functions like func_804211B4 to a kar_* convention?
Did later decomp work recover real names for func_80382400, func_80420FBC, func_804211B4, func_804212F4, or func_80382854?
Did class.h later gain declarations for hsdIsDescendantOf, hsdObjIsDescendantOf, hsdForgetClassLibrary, and the reporting helpers added in class.c?
Were the forced-active class diagnostic strings and lbl_804D7708 later renamed with better semantic names?
